text: Well, I told you souls don't get much
bigger than this. Iron Heart has finally

Having suffered through the first three
episodes now, I can confidently say that

have ever produced in nearly 20 years of
MCU history. And damn, man, that's up
against some pretty stiff competition by
this point. A cringe-inducing calamity
of a show drenched in identity politics
that have aged worse than Mickey Ror's
face.
Weighed down by hammy acting and
nonsensical dialogue. A protagonist
who's willing to lie, cheat, and steal
just to repeat something that's already
been done decades ago. A moral compass
that changes depending on who it's
applied to. and characters so
obnoxiously cliched and stereotypical
that they feel like a parody of
corporate social justice programs. In
short, Iron Hart is a creative, logical,
and probably financial disaster and the
worst possible thing that could happen
to Marvel in a year when it's already
staring down the barrel of three
high-profile flops. Let's get this over

Riri Williams. a grad student at MIT

[ __ ] cares. Watching the first three
episodes of this absolute dross was like
some weird exercise in self-
flagagillation. It truly is something
unique in the MCU, combined in the worst
aspects of everything we've come to hate
about this franchise. the pandering and
heavy-handed racial politics of Falcon
and the Winter Soldier. The tright and
predictable girl bossing of the Marvels
and Wakanda Forever, the moral
flexibility of Black Widow, where doing
horrible things is just fine if you
happen to tick the right diversity
boxes, and the cringe-inducing attempts
at humor of well, everything they've
done since Endgame really. This thing
makes Robin in the Hood look like a
masterpiece of urban fantasy. Parker's
Gang is the most laughable collection of
hapless retards I've seen assembled on
screen since Zack Snder last made a
movie. Riri has all the charm and
likability of Rachel Zgler, only without
the singing ability. The show even makes
a point of her not wanting to diss Tony
Stark, implying that she actually could
and should verbally destroy him, but
chooses not to in her magnanimous
wisdom.
A and [ __ ] me. I don't know what kind of

it can't possibly be worth it to appear

because of course he is. But he doesn't

which is why he's become a black market

absolutely tell this show was
commissioned during a very different
time in American culture. From the
murals of dead community members to the
Black Lives Matter posters to the
constant reminders that things are just
so much harder for people like Riri
because of how she was born. It's
actually kind of crazy now to think that
stuff like this was once shockingly
close to becoming the norm in cinema.
But that was then and this is now. and
the cultural zeitgeist is well and truly
moved on, leaving behind shows like Iron
Hart as dated relics of a time most
people would rather forget. If there's
anyone from Marvel who happens to be
watching this video and you're wondering
why all your new projects are failing
and your franchise is falling apart,
well, the answer is right here in front

worst of everything audiences dislike
about your brand now, and it's the
reason you're probably going to have

I don't know what it would have cost to

Anyway, that's all I've got for today.
